art_highlights: Global art sales reached $59.6 billion in 2025, up 4%, showing signs of stabilization.
US market leads with 44% share; strong resurgence in Old Masters (up 30%) and Modern art (up 9%).
Shift toward tangible assets and established provenance as dealers prioritize risk-averse, high-quality works.
Middle East region emerging as a primary global hub for 2026 with new institutional openings.

market_trends.summary: Cautious optimism for 2026 with a structural shift toward established categories and in-person experiences over speculative online-only sales.
key_points: Online-only sales declined to 15% of the market; art fair sales rose to 35% of dealer turnover.
High-end market ($10M+) is the primary driver of growth in the US.
Younger collectors increasingly active in digital art and photography segments.
Dealer confidence remains polarized, with high operational costs challenging the mid-market gallery sector.
